latest.description: Deployment of Scalable Web Infrastructure & IAM Security Framework

latest.title: The Free Platform ALL Majors Are Sleeping On
latest.description: Most students outside of CS have never heard of AWS Builder Center, and that gap is quietly costing them career leverage before they even graduate.

latest.title: Govern MCP Tools at Scale with Kiro and AgentCore Gateway
latest.description: Learn to govern MCP tools at scale using Kiro and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway with identity-aware authentication and Cedar policy enforcement.

latest.title: What I Wish I Knew Before Learning AWS as a College Student
latest.description: Starting AWS can feel overwhelming. Here are the key things I wish I knew earlier that would have saved me time and helped me build real projects faster.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist:  Social Ace - Unlocking Connection for Autistic Youth through AI on AWS
latest.description: Social Ace is an AI-driven social skills trainer transforming how autistic youth practice communication. As a Top 50 Finalist in the AWS 10,000 AIdeas Challenge, this project combines gamified dialogue with a high-performance AWS architecture—including Bedrock AgentCore and Nova Sonic 2. Our goal is simple: to provide an affordable, engaging, and secure "Social Gymnasium" where every child can build the confidence to thrive in the real world.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: NeuroVoice
latest.description: Small moments, repeated daily, reveal what single snapshots never can - patterns that quietly signal neurological risk. NeuroVoice is a multimodal Parkinson’s screening platform that tracks pattern over time to detect subtle neurological changes early.

latest.title: AWS Unilorin Project (Week 1–3): EC2 + IAM + Security + Billing
latest.description: This project showcases my practical implementation of IAM and EC2 services on AWS, including access control, server deployment, security configuration, billing monitoring, and permission validation through hands-on learning

latest.title: Week 2 of My Cloud Computing Journey: Exploring AWS Compute Services
latest.description: Week 2 of my cloud journey focused on AWS Compute Services, where I explored Amazon EC2, learned to launch and connect to a Linux server via SSH, and understood key concepts like security groups, key pairs, and cost management. A practical step forward in building my cloud and security foundation.

latest.title: Why more instructions make your AI agent worse - Three principles for reliable agent behavior
latest.description: Every rule you add to your agent's system prompt competes for its attention - and prohibitions actively teach the wrong behavior. Three principles and a layered architecture for writing fewer, better agent instructions.

latest.title: Kiro Quickstarts for Amazon Quick: AI-Assisted Development That Actually Works
latest.description: Six open-source projects with working scripts, tested patterns, and steering files that give Kiro the context it needs to help you automate Amazon Quick APIs — from Q-Topics to cross-account migrations.

latest.title: Fine-tuning RoBERTa-base model for Semantic Similarity with Amazon SageMaker AI
latest.description: This blog demonstrates how to fine-tune the HuggingFace RoBERTa-base model on the STS-B semantic similarity task using Amazon SageMaker AI.

latest.title: The Harness Pattern: What Happens When You Stop Defining Your Agents
latest.description: Learn about the new pattern to define agents, how it works and how it can be used in a biotech researcher agent

latest.title: How We Built a Virtual Team of 10+ AI Agents
latest.description:
Last Monday evening, over 300 people walked into the AWS office in Singapore for the largest OpenClaw community meetup in Southeast Asia. Engineers, founders, C-suite leaders, students — all in the same room. About half had never attended before.

We were two of the speakers. Annie shared how she runs a team of 10+ AI agents through Telegram. Melanie presented the technical architecture for deploying OpenClaw safely on AWS at scale. This blog is our attempt to put the full story down in writing.

latest.title: Investigate Azure Infrastructure Failures with AWS DevOps Agent
latest.description: Walk through the complete flow to set up the AWS DevOps Agent integration with Azure, deploy a sample banking API, introduce a PostgreSQL network failure, and launch an autonomous cross-cloud investigation that identifies the root cause in minutes — with no stored credentials or agents in Azure.

latest.title: Automação de Testes Web com Amazon Nova Act e Kiro IDE
latest.description: Criando testes de interfaces web usando o Amazon Nova Act

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: Ivy - The World's First Offline-Capable, Proactive AI Tutoring Agent
latest.description: A proactive AI tutoring agent powered by Amazon Bedrock that monitors student progress, initiates outbound voice calls, and runs on-device inference for offline learning addressing educational inequity for millions of Ethiopian students with intermittent connectivity.

latest.title: Conquering Week 3 of the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp: EC2, IAM, Security, and Billing
latest.description: Welcome to my recap of Week 3 of the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026! This week was an incredibly hands-on experience where we dove into some of the most fundamental and crucial services in AWS: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Identity and Access Management (IAM), Security Groups, and Billing.

latest.title: Securing the Cloud: My Week 3 AWS Bootcamp Deep Dive into EC2, IAM, and Billing
latest.description:
Just published a deep dive into my Week 3 progress with the AWS Cloud Club Unilorin Bootcamp 2026! This week was all about moving beyond basic setups to implement production-ready security and governance.

Check out the full article to see the terminal configurations and the final live deployment. ☁️ #AWS #CloudComputing #DevOps

latest.title: AWS Bootcamp (UNILORIN)
latest.description: This article provides a brief, hands-on overview of essential cloud setup tasks on Amazon Web Services. It walks through cost monitoring, identity management, secure server deployment, and basic application hosting using Amazon EC2.

trending.description: As a developer facing email overload, I built a solution for the Q Developer Challenge #1: email management. What began as a simple CLI tool grew into an AI-powered system that has transformed my workflow.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Caligo Dynamics - AI Powered Autonomous Drone Swarm for Emergency Response
trending.description: When a fire breaks out, firefighters often enter buildings blindly, unable to see victims, hazards, or even the way out. This project uses AI-powered drone swarms built with ROS2, PX4, Kiro, and Amazon Bedrock to detect fires, locate civilians, map dangerous environments, and help emergency teams contain fires before lives are lost.
